roadmap.strategy: One-at-a-time DELIVER: each step enables exactly ONE acceptance scenario and drives one outside-in TDD cycle (RED acceptance -> RED_UNIT pure-core -> GREEN -> REFACTOR). Scenario->step spine is docs/feature/htmx-scraper-viewer/distill/acceptance-review.md. The walking skeleton (01-01) is necessarily the thickest step: it forces the two new crates and three extended crates into existence end-to-end. The gold invariants (Phase 06) are LAST by design -- several flip green for free once the routes they guard exist; for those, the step is 'remove the @skip / confirm already-green' if the structural invariant holds.
roadmap.build_before_run: Acceptance tests spawn the REAL openlore bin via ViewerServer (assert_cmd::cargo_bin, runtime-resolved). The run MUST `cargo build` the openlore bin BEFORE running viewer ATs so ViewerServer spawns the CURRENT `openlore ui`, not a stale one (slice-05 indexer precedent). `reqwest` is a new cli dev-dependency (0.12, rustls) for the in-test HTTP client.
global_invariants: READ-ONLY (I-VIEW-1/2): no route writes or signs; web process holds StoreReadPort only -- no write StoragePort, no IdentityPort, no PdsPort, no signing key.

derived-from honesty (I-VIEW-5 / WD-62): derived-from renders ONLY on /scrape candidate rows; persisted view-models (ClaimRowView/ClaimDetailView/PeerClaimRowView) have NO derived-from slot -- the mistake is unrepresentable.
offline (I-VIEW-6 / KPI-VIEW-5): store views (/claims, /claims/{cid}, /peer-claims) work fully offline; only /scrape touches the network.
loopback only (I-VIEW-4): hard-coded 127.0.0.1 bind, no --host; loopback self-probe refuses otherwise.
human sign gate stays in CLI (I-VIEW-3 / I-SCR-1): no sign control on any rendered page.
same store (BR-VIEW-4 / WD-8): reads the SAME single-file DuckDB the CLI writes, over the SAME shared Arc<Mutex<Connection>>; zero new persisted types, zero new table, zero new CID path.
confidence verbatim (FR-VIEW-8): confidence rendered from the stored f64, never rounded/reformatted.

phases.name: Store list view + read-only startup
phases.description: Stand the viewer up end-to-end over the operator's own claims: the walking skeleton forces the two new crates (viewer-domain, adapter-http-viewer) and three extended crates (ports, adapter-duckdb, cli) into existence, then layers the read-only launch notice, the empty-state, and the startup store-readability refusal. After this phase, `openlore ui` binds loopback, serves GET /claims over the REAL DuckDB, and refuses to start (health.startup.refused) when the store is unreadable.

steps.name: WALKING SKELETON: operator sees their signed claims in the browser with zero SQL
steps.description: The thinnest end-to-end slice that proves the architecture outside-in. Given Maria signed ('rust-lang/rust','is-maintained-by','The Rust Project') @0.90 via the CLI, when she starts `openlore ui` and opens My Claims, then she sees that row (subject/predicate/object/confidence 0.90 verbatim/CID) rendered as HTML -- no SQL. Forces into existence: the viewer-domain pure crate skeleton + render_claims_page (maud); the adapter-http-viewer hyper serve loop + loopback bind + GET /claims handler; StoreReadPort in ports + the ClaimRow/Page/PageRequest boundary types; the adapter-duckdb StoreReadPort impl (list_claims/count_claims over the shared handle); the `openlore ui` verb in cli wiring the read-only handle + reusing build_tokio_runtime; the xtask allowlist (maud) + viewer-domain pure-core arm + viewer capability rule; and the `viewer.serve.listening` stdout event emitted on bind. Materializes the ViewerServer test-harness body + seed_own_claim_with_evidence helper.
acceptance_criteria:
Starting `openlore ui --port

` binds 127.0.0.1:

and emits a `viewer.serve.listening` event on stdout naming the bound loopback URL.

A GET /claims request returns 200 and an HTML body containing the seeded claim's subject 'rust-lang/rust', predicate 'is-maintained-by', object 'The Rust Project', the CID, and the confidence rendered verbatim as '0.90' (not '0.9', not '90%').
The rendered page is HTML (the operator reads it in a browser); the scenario never issues SQL.
The claims read comes from the REAL DuckDB seeded through the production `claim add` write path (BR-VIEW-4): same store the CLI writes.
viewer-domain depends on maud + ports only -- no duckdb/tokio/reqwest/hyper/keyring/atrium (enforced by xtask check-arch pure-core arm).
adapter-http-viewer holds a Box<dyn StoreReadPort> with no write/sign method; cli is the only crate that links adapter-http-viewer (xtask invariant 5).

new_crates: adapter-http-viewer (EFFECT: hyper serve loop + GET /claims handler + probe skeleton)
extended_crates: xtask (maud allowlist + viewer-domain pure-core arm + viewer capability rule)
implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: viewer-domain::render_claims_page over PageView<ClaimRowView> -- load-bearing for the Phase-05 mutation gate.

steps.mutation_note: Adds the first pure-core render surface (render_claims_page). Establishes the viewer-domain in-crate unit/property tests that the >=80% mutation gate will hold accountable; the confidence-verbatim rendering branch is a prime survivor target.

steps.name: Launch states it is read-only and reports its loopback URL with no key loaded
steps.description: Given Maria has a local store, when she starts `openlore ui`, then the launch reports the loopback listen URL and states the viewer is read-only, and no signing key is loaded. Forces the read-only launch notice + the loopback URL line + the startup probe wiring (ADR-030 Earned-Trust steps 2/3: read-only-capability self-probe + loopback-bind self-probe run before the serve loop, WIRE->PROBE->USE).
acceptance_criteria: On launch, stdout reports the bound URL and it is a loopback address (127.0.0.0/8 or ::1).
The launch output and the landing page (GET /) state the viewer is read-only.
No signing key, IdentityPort, or PdsPort is loaded by the ui verb wiring (structural: ViewerDeps holds only StoreReadPort + GithubPort).
The loopback self-probe refuses to start if the bound address is not loopback (I-VIEW-4).

implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: viewer-domain layout/shell helper renders the read-only banner.

steps.mutation_note: Read-only banner + loopback-derived URL string are pure-core; assert the banner text and loopback formatting are pinned to kill string-mutation survivors.

steps.name: Empty store guides the operator to the CLI instead of showing a blank page
steps.description: Given Tom signed nothing, when he opens My Claims, then he sees guidance ('claims you sign with the CLI appear here'), not a blank page. Forces render_empty(NoClaims) and the EmptyState::NoClaims view-model wiring on the /claims route when total == 0.
acceptance_criteria: With zero claims in the store, GET /claims returns 200 with a guided empty-state message directing the operator to sign claims with the CLI.
The empty page renders no pagination controls and no error/stack trace.
The store row count is unchanged by rendering the empty view (read-only).

extended_crates: adapter-http-viewer (claims handler dispatches to render_empty when total == 0)
implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: viewer-domain::render_empty(NoClaims).

steps.mutation_note: render_empty is pure; the NoClaims guided-message branch and the total==0 dispatch boundary are mutation targets (off-by-one on the empty/non-empty fork).

steps.name: Unreadable store refuses startup in plain language instead of crashing per request
steps.description: Given the store file is locked by another process, when the operator starts the viewer, then the viewer refuses to start with a plain-language message naming the path and 'another process', no stack trace. Forces the startup store-readability refusal (ADR-030 Earned-Trust step 1): the probe runs a sentinel count_claims() and, on failure, emits a structured `health.startup.refused` event and a plain-language stderr line. Materializes run_openlore_ui_expecting_startup_refusal in the harness.
acceptance_criteria: When the store is unreadable (file locked by another process), `openlore ui` exits without binding a serve loop.
A structured `health.startup.refused` event is emitted with the reason (store unreadable).
The stderr message is plain language, names the store path, mentions 'another process', and contains NO stack trace and NO raw error/exception type.
The refusal happens at startup (probe-before-serve), not as a per-request 500.

extended_crates: cli (health.startup.refused emission + plain-language stderr; port-in-use suggests --port)
implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: viewer-domain::render_error / plain-language cause formatting (no stack trace).

steps.mutation_note: The probe pass/refuse fork and the no-stack-trace message construction are mutation targets; pin that the refusal path names the path + omits transport internals.

phases.name: Claim detail view
phases.description: Drill into a single own claim: full fields plus its evidence array, including the no-evidence state and the guided not-found for an unknown CID. Adds StoreReadPort::get_claim + render_claim_detail.

steps.name: Operator inspects one claim and sees all its evidence
steps.description: Given a claim has two evidence URLs, when the operator opens its detail page, then they see all fields plus both evidence URLs. Forces StoreReadPort::get_claim (row + claim_evidence joined by cid, ordered by ordinal) + the ClaimDetail boundary type + render_claim_detail (with the evidence[] section).
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims/{cid} for an existing claim returns 200 with subject, predicate, object, confidence verbatim, author_did, composed_at, and CID.
Both evidence URLs render, one per row, in ordinal order.
Confidence is rendered verbatim from the stored f64 (FR-VIEW-8).
The detail read is read-only over the same DuckDB store; row counts unchanged.

extended_crates: adapter-duckdb (get_claim: row by cid + claim_evidence ORDER BY ordinal)
implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: viewer-domain::render_claim_detail including the evidence list.

steps.mutation_note: render_claim_detail's evidence-iteration and field rendering are pure-core; evidence ordering and the per-field render branches are mutation targets.

steps.name: Claim with no evidence shows a clear note, not a blank section
steps.description: Given a claim signed without evidence, when the operator opens its detail, then the evidence section reads 'no evidence attached', not a blank section. Forces the empty-evidence branch of render_claim_detail (ClaimDetailView with an empty evidence vec).
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims/{cid} for a claim with no evidence returns 200 and renders 'no evidence attached' in the evidence section.
The page renders the claim's other fields normally; the evidence area is not blank/absent.
Read-only: row counts unchanged.

implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: viewer-domain::render_claim_detail empty-evidence path.

steps.mutation_note: The empty-vs-non-empty evidence fork is a direct mutation target; pin the 'no evidence attached' literal and the branch condition.

steps.name: Unknown CID guides the operator back instead of erroring opaquely
steps.description: Given a CID not in the store, when the operator opens that detail page, then they see 'No claim with that identifier in your store' plus a back link. Forces the get_claim -> None path mapped to render_error (guided 404 + next-step link).
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims/{cid} for an unknown CID returns 404 with a plain-language message 'No claim with that identifier in your store'.
The page includes a back link to the claims list.
No stack trace, no raw DB error, no leaked internals.
Read-only: row counts unchanged.

implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: viewer-domain::render_error (cause + next_step, no stack trace).

steps.mutation_note: render_error and the Some/None dispatch are pure-core/handler mutation targets; pin the 404 status mapping and the back-link presence.

phases.name: Peer claims view
phases.description: Show federated peer claims on a distinct route with peer origin, including the no-peers empty state and the defensive unknown-origin render. Adds StoreReadPort::list_peer_claims/count_peer_claims + render_peer_claims_page.

steps.name: Operator sees federated peer claims distinct from their own, with peer origin
steps.description: Given Maria federated peer claims (and has her own), when she opens Peer Claims, then the federated rows show peer origin (author_did) on a route distinct from her own claims. Forces StoreReadPort::list_peer_claims/count_peer_claims + the PeerClaimRow boundary type + render_peer_claims_page (origin from author_did + fetched_from_pds). Materializes seed_peer_claims_via_pull in the harness.
acceptance_criteria: GET /peer-claims returns 200 listing federated peer claims with subject, predicate, object, confidence verbatim, peer origin (author_did), fetched_from_pds, composed_at, and CID.
Peer claims render on a SEPARATE route from /claims; own vs federated is never ambiguous (BR-VIEW-5).
Each peer row projects author_did verbatim (attribution discipline carried forward).
Read-only over the same store; row counts unchanged.

implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: viewer-domain::render_peer_claims_page with the peer-origin column.

steps.mutation_note: render_peer_claims_page peer-origin rendering and the author_did projection are mutation targets; pin that origin renders and is not elided.

steps.name: No federated claims yet shows guidance, not a blank page
steps.description: Given the operator federated nothing, when they open Peer Claims, then they see 'No federated claims yet' guidance. Forces render_empty(NoPeers) on the /peer-claims route when total == 0.
acceptance_criteria: With zero peer claims, GET /peer-claims returns 200 with a guided 'No federated claims yet' message.
No pagination controls and no error are rendered.
Read-only: row counts unchanged.

extended_crates: adapter-http-viewer (peer handler dispatches render_empty when total == 0)
implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: viewer-domain::render_empty(NoPeers).

steps.mutation_note: The NoPeers empty-state branch and the total==0 fork are mutation targets.

steps.name: Peer claim with missing origin still renders as 'unknown', never dropped
steps.description: Given a peer claim has blank/absent origin, when the operator opens Peer Claims, then that claim still renders with origin 'unknown' (not dropped). Forces PeerOrigin::Unknown defensive render. Materializes seed_peer_claim_with_blank_origin in the harness.
acceptance_criteria: A peer claim with blank/absent author_did still appears in the /peer-claims list (row count of rendered claims includes it).
Its origin renders as 'unknown' rather than the row being dropped or the page erroring.
All other fields of the claim render normally.
Read-only: row counts unchanged.

implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: viewer-domain peer-origin render over PeerOrigin (Known/Unknown ADT).

steps.mutation_note: The Known/Unknown ADT match is a high-value mutation target; pin that Unknown renders 'unknown' and the row is never filtered out.

phases.name: Pagination across large stores
phases.description: Paginate the list routes with offset/limit, fixed page size 50, deterministic sort: the 'X-Y of N' indicator and ?page=N, last-page bounds (no Next), and the single-page case (no controls). Adds PageRequest/Page<T> + the PageView indicator.

steps.name: Operator pages through a large claim list with a clear position indicator
steps.description: Given 312 claims at page size 50, when the operator opens My Claims and goes to page 2, then they see '1-50 of 312' then '51-100 of 312'. Forces PageRequest/Page<T> offset/limit (size 50, ORDER BY composed_at DESC, cid ASC) + the PageView 'X-Y of N' indicator + the ?page=N query param + Next/Prev links. Materializes seed_own_claims_via_cli in the harness.
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims (page 1) over 312 claims renders at most 50 rows and the indicator '1-50 of 312'.
GET /claims?page=2 renders the indicator '51-100 of 312' and a different set of 50 rows.
Page boundaries are deterministic across requests (ORDER BY composed_at DESC, cid ASC).
start/end/total math: start = (page-1)*50+1, end = min(page*50, total), total = COUNT(*).
Read-only: row counts unchanged across paging.

implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: viewer-domain PageView arithmetic (start/end/prev/next) + indicator render -- the load-bearing pure pagination surface for the >=80% gate.

steps.mutation_note: Pagination arithmetic (start/end/prev/next, off-by-one boundaries) is the single richest mutation surface in viewer-domain. Add in-crate property tests over (total, page, page_size) so the >=80% mutation gate has live oracles.

steps.name: On the last page, the operator sees the final range and no Next link
steps.description: Given the operator is on the last page of 312, then they see '301-312 of 312' and no link to page 8. Forces the last-page bounds: next = None when end >= total; page clamped to [1, ceil(total/page_size)].
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims?page=7 over 312 claims renders the indicator '301-312 of 312'.
No Next link/control is rendered on the last page (end >= total).
A Prev link IS rendered (not page 1).
Requesting a page beyond the last is clamped to the last page rather than erroring.
Read-only: row counts unchanged.

implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: viewer-domain PageView next/clamp boundary arithmetic.

steps.mutation_note: end>=total -> next=None and the clamp ceiling are prime off-by-one mutation targets; property tests from 04-01 should already cover, assert the bound explicitly.

steps.name: A small store shows a count but no pagination controls
steps.description: Given 12 claims at page size 50, when the operator opens My Claims, then they see '1-12 of 12' and no pagination controls. Forces the single-page case: total <= page_size renders no prev/next.
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims over 12 claims renders all 12 rows and the indicator '1-12 of 12'.
No pagination controls (no Prev, no Next) are rendered when total <= page_size.
Read-only: row counts unchanged.

extended_crates: viewer-domain (single-page no-controls branch in PageView render)
implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: viewer-domain PageView single-page suppression branch.

steps.mutation_note: The total<=page_size 'render no controls' fork is a mutation target; pin both that the count shows and that prev/next are absent.

phases.name: Live scrape proposal view
phases.description: Add the ephemeral /scrape view: a target form + live propose reusing the slice-02 GithubPort + derive_candidates, rendering candidates with derived-from and NO sign control, plus the zero-candidates state and the network-down render (plain language, no transport internals). Persists nothing, signs nothing.

steps.name: Operator browses live scrape proposals with provenance, but cannot sign from the web
steps.description: Given a live scrape of 'rust-lang/cargo' proposes candidates (FakeGithub), when Maria submits that target, then candidates render (subject/predicate/object/confidence + derived-from), the page states 'nothing signed/saved', there is NO sign control, and she is directed to the CLI. Forces the GET /scrape form + POST /scrape live propose (reuse GithubPort resolve+harvest + scraper_domain::derive_candidates) + render_scrape_page with candidates + derived-from, no sign control. Materializes ViewerServer::start_with_github wiring the reused slice-02 FakeGithub via OPENLORE_GITHUB_API_BASE.
acceptance_criteria: GET /scrape returns 200 with a labeled target form (no candidates).
POST /scrape with target 'rust-lang/cargo' returns 200 rendering candidate rows with subject, predicate, object, confidence, and a derived-from provenance value on each candidate.
The page states nothing was signed or saved and directs the operator to the CLI to sign.
NO sign/save control is rendered anywhere on the page (I-SCR-1).
POST /scrape persists nothing: claims and peer_claims row counts are unchanged (BR-VIEW-2 / I-VIEW-1).
The harvest reuses the slice-02 GithubPort/FakeGithub double via OPENLORE_GITHUB_API_BASE -- no new GitHub double built.

extended_crates: adapter-http-viewer (GET/POST /scrape handlers; depend on scraper-domain for derive_candidates; take GithubPort as Box<dyn>)
viewer-domain (CandidateRowView -- the ONLY view-model with derived_from -- + ScrapeState + render_scrape_page, no sign control)
cli (ui verb builds GithubPort via GithubAdapter::from_env and injects it)
implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: viewer-domain::render_scrape_page (candidates + derived-from + 'nothing signed/saved' guidance, NO sign affordance).

steps.mutation_note: render_scrape_page candidate rendering + the derived-from presence branch + the no-sign-control guarantee are mutation targets. CandidateRowView is the only type carrying derived_from -- assert it renders here and that the page has no sign affordance.

steps.name: A target that derives no candidates explains why and suggests an alternative
steps.description: Given 'some-org/empty-repo' derives no candidates, when the operator submits it, then they see 'No candidate claims could be derived' plus a suggested alternative. Forces render_empty(NoCandidates) / ScrapeState::ZeroCandidates on the /scrape route.
acceptance_criteria: POST /scrape for a target that yields zero candidates returns 200 with 'No candidate claims could be derived' and a suggested alternative.
No candidate rows and no sign control are rendered.
Persists nothing: row counts unchanged.

implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: viewer-domain::render_empty(NoCandidates) / ZeroCandidates render.

steps.mutation_note: The empty-candidates fork and the guided-message literal are mutation targets.

steps.name: When GitHub is unreachable, the operator gets a plain-language note and the offline reassurance
steps.description: Given GitHub is unreachable (FakeGithub::offline()), when the operator submits 'tokio-rs/tokio', then they see 'GitHub could not be reached' plus 'store view still works offline', with NO leaked transport internals. Forces the NetworkDown render (ScrapeState::NetworkDown): plain-language cause + offline note, no HTTP status, no 'connection refused'/'timed out'/'DNS', no raw URL, no stack trace (the DESIGN nit resolution, NFR-VIEW-6/7).
acceptance_criteria: POST /scrape when GitHub is unreachable returns a page naming the cause in domain language: 'GitHub could not be reached'.
The page reassures that the offline store view still works (NFR-VIEW-7).
The render leaks NO transport internals: no HTTP status code, no 'connection refused'/'timed out'/'DNS', no raw URL, no stack trace (NFR-VIEW-6).
Persists nothing: row counts unchanged; the store views remain reachable.

extended_crates: viewer-domain (ScrapeState::NetworkDown render: plain-language cause + offline note, no transport detail)
adapter-http-viewer (map GithubPort network failure -> NetworkDown state, sanitizing the cause)
implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: viewer-domain NetworkDown render (the DESIGN nit resolution).

steps.mutation_note: The network-failure -> NetworkDown mapping and the transport-sanitization (no status/refused/DNS/URL) are mutation targets; assert both the plain-language cause AND the absence of leaked internals.

phases.name: Gold invariants (read-only / honesty / offline / no-key)
phases.description: The behavioral guardrails, LAST by design (ADR-030 Earned-Trust step 2/3 + the acceptance-review one-at-a-time discipline). Several flip green for free once the routes they guard exist -- the correct signal that the structural invariants were designed in, not bolted on. For each, the step may be 'remove the @skip / confirm already-green' if the structural invariant holds; otherwise it adds the missing guardrail surface (capture_store_row_count_universe / assert_store_read_only helpers).

steps.name: Gold: the viewer is read-only across every route, including POST /scrape
steps.description: Given a store seeded (own + peer) and a reachable target, when EVERY route incl. POST /scrape is exercised, then claims + peer_claims row counts are UNCHANGED (universe-bound assert_store_read_only). Guards I-VIEW-1 (read-only). Likely 'remove the @skip / confirm already-green' once all routes exist, since the StoreReadPort holds no mutation method by construction. Materializes capture_store_row_count_universe + assert_store_read_only over the port-exposed universe {claims.row_count, peer_claims.row_count}.
acceptance_criteria: After exercising every route (GET /, /claims, /claims/{cid}, /peer-claims, GET/POST /scrape), the {claims.row_count, peer_claims.row_count} universe is identical to before.
The assertion uses the port-exposed universe names, never internal adapter fields (Mandate 8).
No route exposes a write/sign affordance.
If the invariant already holds (it should, structurally), the step is to remove the @skip and confirm GREEN -- not to add new production logic.

extended_crates: (expected: none -- already-green confirmation; if a leak is found, the fix lands in adapter-http-viewer / cli wiring, not viewer-domain)
implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: none (behavioral guardrail over the driving port).

steps.mutation_note: Guardrail, not a new pure surface. If it goes red, the regression is in a handler that mutates -- the fix, not the test, carries the mutation accountability.

steps.name: Gold: derived-from appears only on live candidates, never on persisted claims
steps.description: Given own + peer persisted + a live target, when the live view and every persisted view are rendered, then derived-from is present on candidates and ABSENT on every persisted view. Guards I-VIEW-5 / WD-62. Largely green once V-S1 + the persisted view-models (which have no derived-from slot) exist -- may be 'remove the @skip' if the type-level invariant holds (the mistake is unrepresentable).
acceptance_criteria: POST /scrape renders derived-from on candidate rows.
GET /claims, GET /claims/{cid}, and GET /peer-claims render NO derived-from anywhere.
The absence is structural: persisted view-models (ClaimRowView/ClaimDetailView/PeerClaimRowView) have no derived_from slot.
If the type-level invariant holds, the step is to remove the @skip and confirm GREEN.

extended_crates: (expected: none -- already-green via the type-level invariant)
implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: none (behavioral guardrail; the structural guarantee lives in the viewer-domain ADTs already).

steps.mutation_note: Guardrail. The honesty contract is enforced at the type level (no derived_from slot on persisted view-models); the gold test proves it, mutation accountability rests on the view-model ADT shape from Phases 01-03.

steps.name: Gold: the store views render fully offline
steps.description: Given the network is unavailable and own + peer are persisted, when the store views are rendered, then each renders fully from the local store. Guards I-VIEW-6 / KPI-VIEW-5. Likely green once the store routes exist -- offline by construction (store reads touch only local DuckDB) -- may be 'remove the @skip'.
acceptance_criteria: With the network unavailable, GET /claims, GET /claims/{cid}, and GET /peer-claims each return 200 and render their full content from the local store.
No store route makes a network call.
If offline-by-construction holds, the step is to remove the @skip and confirm GREEN.

implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: none (behavioral guardrail over the store routes).

steps.mutation_note: Guardrail. If red, a store handler reached the network -- the fix removes that dependency; the test pins offline reachability.

steps.name: Gold: the web process holds no signing key and no route can sign
steps.description: Given a populated store and the viewer running, when every route is requested, then no sign control appears on any page and store row counts are unchanged (no write/sign). Guards I-VIEW-1/2 / I-SCR-1. Largely green once the no-sign-control surfaces + the read-only delta exist -- may be 'remove the @skip'. Reinforced structurally by the xtask viewer-capability rule (adapter-http-viewer transitive deps exclude adapter-atproto-pds and no IdentityPort signing surface).
acceptance_criteria: Requesting every route renders NO sign/save control on any page.
Store row counts are unchanged across all requests (no write/sign occurred).

The xtask check-arch viewer-capability rule confirms adapter-http-viewer's transitive deps exclude adapter-atproto-pds and reach no signing IdentityPort surface; cli remains the only crate linking adapter-http-viewer.
If the structural guarantees hold, the step is to remove the @skip and confirm GREEN.

extended_crates: (otherwise: none -- structural no-key guarantee already in place)
implementation_scope.pure_core_surface: none (behavioral + structural guardrail).

steps.mutation_note: Guardrail backed by the xtask capability rule (the structural orthogonal layer per principle 12). The behavioral gold test + the check-arch rule + Rust's type check (no write port in ViewerDeps) form the three-layer Earned-Trust defense; no new pure surface.

new_crates: crates/viewer-domain (PURE: view-model ADTs + render_* via maud)
crates/adapter-http-viewer (EFFECT: hyper server + route table + real probe)
extended_crates: crates/adapter-duckdb (StoreReadPort impl over the shared Arc<Mutex<Connection>>)
crates/cli (the `ui` verb composition root, reuse build_tokio_runtime)
crates/xtask (maud allowlist + viewer-domain pure-core arm + viewer capability rule)
external_integrations: GitHub public API via the reused slice-02 GithubPort (adapter-github), only on POST /scrape. Contract-test candidacy already covered by ADR-019. The store-read boundary is purely local (DuckDB) -- no external integration.
